title: AI Strategy for Education
description: A strategy course for K-12 and higher-education stakeholders on building and deploying an AI strategy. It is grounded in the METR finding that AI capability doubles every four to seven months, and structured around a six-stage idea funnel: gathering ideas, an idea registry, evaluating ideas, selecting projects, assigning resources, and evaluating projects. It plans against the near-term arrival of ~10,000 intelligent textbooks with xAPI telemetry, AI-recommended learning plans by 2028, the Alpha School model, and a near-term agentic workforce in which every administrator, teacher, and student manages dozens of named AI agents — with a balanced analysis of AI risk and reward.
creator: Dan McCreary
